title: Referential Integrity During Bulk Load Procedures
breadcrumbs: Referential Integrity During Bulk Load Procedures
text: Referential Integrity During Bulk Load Procedures Referential integrity may be violated during bulk load procedures such as importing and exporting data, so the RDBMS implementation of referential integrity must be disabled. However, to ensure that Cascade and Nullify foreign key rules are enforced when required, and the Uniface implementation must be enabled. The connector should support a connector option to control this behavior. The Uniface bulk load procedure (used in /cpy , /imp , and so on) does the following: Drops the referential integrity constraints and triggers in the database (using SQL generated in Open mode 5). Enables the Uniface implementation of foreign key rules using a connector option. Performs the bulk load. Checks the database (using the SQL generated in Open mode 6). When problems are encountered, the records violating referential integrity have to be corrected. Repeat this step until no more errors occur. Disables the Uniface implementation of foreign key rules (let the RDBMS enforce the rules). Re-creates the referential integrity constraints and triggers in the database (using SQL generated in Open mode 4). Connector Option By default, Uniface handles referential integrity checking, using its own implementation of Cascade and Nullify delete constraints. For some databases, it is possible for the database itself to handle referential integrity checking. Depending which approach is use, it is recommended that the connector should support a connector option to either: Disable the Uniface implementation (if it is enabled by default) Enable the Uniface implementation (if it is disabled by default) The option can be set in the assignment file using the setting USYS$ XXX _PARAMS , where XXX is the connector mnemonic. The service function udgetasn can be used to read the setting from the assignment file. When the connector option is read, use the noconst flag in the uinfo structure during the info request to control or disable the CAS and NUL foreign key rules for deleting the one table. Perform a bitwise OR with the value 4 for the following options: 0—enabled 3—disabled Related Links udgetasn Find the specified assignment and copy it to the assignment value buffer. uinfo Supplies Uniface with basic information about the DBMS and the connector. Referential Integrity Referential integrity is a standard feature of relational database design that prevents users or applications from entering inconsistent data. Most relational databases have referential integrity rules that you can apply when you create a relationship between tables. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
